label: Uncomplicated plantar fasciitis without trauma or neurovascular red flags
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated plantar fasciitis.
No trauma/fracture concern, infection, open wound, diabetic-foot/PVD/neuropathy risk, neurovascular compromise, inability to walk, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion_criteria: Trauma, fracture/dislocation concern, inability to bear weight, neurovascular compromise, open wound, cellulitis, abscess, fever, or systemic illness.
Diabetic foot risk, neuropathy, PVD, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed podiatry/orthopedic plan.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, podiatry, sports medicine, or orthopedics if pain persists or walking remains limited.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports plantar-fasciitis framing, heel/bottom-foot pain, and supportive measures including rest, stretching, footwear changes, and clinician-directed pain control. Autonomous Phase 1801-1850 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated plantar fasciitis without trauma/fracture concern, infection, diabetic-foot/PVD/neuropathy risk, neurovascular compromise, inability to walk, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. MedlinePlus supports plantar-fasciitis framing, heel/bottom-foot pain, and supportive measures including rest, stretching, footwear changes, and clinician-directed pain control. Autonomous Phase 1801-1850 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated plantar fasciitis without trauma/fracture concern, infection, diabetic-foot/PVD/neuropathy risk, neurovascular compromise, inability to walk, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated plantar fasciitis
no trauma/fracture concern, open wound, diabetic foot, or neurovascular red flags documented
no inability to walk, infection, pregnancy, pediatric, or specialist-directed pathway
